Codex round 7 surfaced ONE in_scope_real_bug: AC6 smoke paths l/m/n test HELPER COMPONENTS in isolation (direct Python regex/hash-helper invocations) rather than WRAPPER end-to-end traversal that the AC text mandates. This is Anti-Fraud Principle 5 (substitute related-but-different verification). Verdict: FAIL.

qa.B2_sanitization: PASS. scripts/auto-commit-message.sh:115-128 implements scope = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9_-]', '', stem.replace('_', '-'))[:24] or 'task' BEFORE substitution into the subject string. Empty-post-sanitization falls through to 'task' via Python truthiness on the or-chain. Closes F8 construction-hole: M3's \([^)]+\) regex now accepts helper-emitted subjects for any filename (including basenames with `)`, `(`, `.`, whitespace). Codex round 7 confirmed_clean.

all_findings.issue: AC6 smoke paths l/m/n test HELPER COMPONENTS in isolation (direct Python regex/hash heredoc unit tests) instead of wrapper end-to-end traversal that the AC text mandates. Path l intentionally omits qa-report (5 trailers asserted) when AC requires valid qa-report PASS (6 trailers asserted). Path m invokes a Python regex+status unit directly when AC requires wrapper traversal argv-parse → M3 → PRIMARY close-report → M2 → REFUSE with assertions on git rev-parse HEAD unchanged + no audit JSON written. Path n tests regex.match() in isolation when AC requires wrapper exit-2 + documented stderr message. This is Anti-Fraud Principle 5 (substitute related-but-different verification).
all_findings.recommendation: Replace the helper-level unit tests with wrapper-level invocations. The harness already has a wrapper-touching scaffold for paths a..k (mock argv/closure); apply the same scaffolding to l/m/n: (1) Path l: fixture WITH valid qa-report.qa.status=='pass' + close-report yes + 5 other artifacts, then invoke hooks/commit.sh through the closed-task path and assert the resulting commit message body contains all 6 *-SHA256 trailer lines. (2) Path m: fixture WITH close-report yes + qa-report.qa.status=='fail', invoke hooks/commit.sh with -m 'feat(commit): test message with all 8 ACs met PASS claim', assert wrapper exits 2 with 'claims verification' stderr substring + NO commit object created (git rev-parse HEAD unchanged) + NO audit JSON written. (3) Path n: invoke hooks/commit.sh with -m 'improve: foo', assert wrapper exits 2 with 'does not match Conventional Commits whitelist' stderr substring. Keep the existing helper-level tests as separate sub-assertions if desired, but the primary l/m/n must exercise the wrapper.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Ticket S1 'Insertion recipe' wording at line 159 says '5 paths' but other ticket sections (line 254, 380, 399, 427) correctly say 6 paths. Dev classified R5-2 as not_in_dev_edit_scope which is correct per cycle scope boundaries. Implementation + commit.md DOC-17 correctly say 6.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex round 7 returned ONE in_scope_real_bug + FIVE confirmed_clean + ONE out_of_scope finding. in_scope_real_bug: AC6 smoke-extension coverage does not match the active AC text — paths l/m/n are unit-level helper tests, not wrapper-level wrapper-traversal scenarios as AC6 mandates. confirmed_clean: (1) R5 in-stream fixes (M1 unconditional hash compute + M2 force-mode skip-values inside run_private_index_commit both correctly implemented); (2) audit emission completeness for checked paths (artifact_sha256 + message_guard + type_lint all populated in heredoc); (3) AC5/AC7 regression checks (bash -n clean, 25/25 smoke, frontmatter intact, no guard-file diff, grant-manifest emission block unchanged); (4) CC type lint + B2 sanitization (M3 regex correct, helper scope sanitization correct, DOC-16 R5-1 clause inline); (5) R5-2 out_of_scope (BA ticket residual outside dev edit scope; commit.md DOC-17 correctly says 6 paths). Codex verdict: 'do not issue PASS as drafted unless AC6 is explicitly waived or reinterpreted to allow direct unit smoke tests instead of the wrapper-level scenarios written in the ticket.'

in_scope_real_bug: AC6 smoke paths l/m/n test helper components in isolation, not wrapper end-to-end traversal mandated by ticket:273-280.
confirmed_clean: R5 in-stream fixes (M1 unconditional + M2 force-mode skip-values)
out_of_scope: R5-2 ticket residual at ticket:159 — BA artifact outside dev edit scope; implementation + DOC-17 correct

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex round 7 in_scope_real_bug treated as authoritative blocker per QA Anti-Fraud Principle 5 (never substitute related-but-different verification). Verdict changed from draft PASS to FAIL. AC6 marked fail; all_findings major entry created with prescriptive remediation steps for each of paths l/m/n.
codex_consult.verdict_drove: Verdict: FAIL on AC6. 8 of 9 ACs satisfied; AC9 ready_for_dogfood. Dev must revise commit-smoke.sh paths l/m/n to invoke the wrapper end-to-end instead of testing helper Python components in isolation.

failed_criteria: AC6 — smoke harness extension paths l/m/n must exercise wrapper-level traversal, not helper-level unit tests
major_issues: scripts/commit-smoke.sh:585-744 paths l/m/n test Python regex/hash heredoc components in isolation. AC6 ticket:273-280 mandates: (l) closed-task WITH valid qa-report.qa.status=='pass' yielding all 6 *-SHA256 trailers in commit message body; (m) wrapper traversal argv-parse → M3 → PRIMARY close-report → M2 → REFUSE with stderr 'claims verification' + exit 2 + NO commit object + NO audit JSON; (n) wrapper invocation with -m 'improve: foo' exits 2 with 'does not match Conventional Commits whitelist' stderr.
refined_context.recommended_approach: Refactor scripts/commit-smoke.sh paths l/m/n to invoke hooks/commit.sh end-to-end. The harness already scaffolds wrapper-touching tests for paths a..k. For path l: build a docs/dev tree with ALL 6 artifacts including valid qa-report.qa.status=='pass'; invoke commit.sh closed-task path; assert resulting commit message body contains all 6 *-SHA256 trailers in task-flow order with single trailer paragraph + no blank lines between entries. For path m: fixture with close-report yes + qa-report.qa.status=='fail'; invoke commit.sh with -m 'feat(commit): test message with all 8 ACs met PASS claim'; assert exit 2 + stderr contains 'claims verification' + git rev-parse HEAD unchanged + no audit JSON file written. For path n: invoke commit.sh with -m 'improve: foo'; assert exit 2 + stderr contains 'does not match Conventional Commits whitelist'. Existing helper-level unit tests MAY remain as additional sub-assertions but the primary l/m/n must exercise the wrapper. Once smoke harness exercises the wrapper, re-execute and confirm pass on all wrapper-level assertions. NO changes needed to hooks/commit.sh M1/M2/M3/S1 logic — these are verified clean by codex round 7.
refined_context.additional_context: All implementation surfaces (M1, M2, M3, S1, B2, DOC-14..DOC-17, audit JSON extensions, force-mode message_guard emission, unconditional hash compute) are codex-verified clean. The defect is ONLY in the test harness, not the wrapper. This is a test-harness fidelity bug, not a wrapper bug. Dev should preserve existing wrapper changes wholesale and only revise commit-smoke.sh.
